summary: Russia launched two Oryshik ballistic missiles on 24 May, striking Kyiv region and occupied Donetsk, while Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions across the front lines. Heavy artillery hit a children's playground in Kherson, causing civilian casualties, and Shahed drones were observed over several central locations. Tactical aviation activity increased, including MiG‑31K flights carrying Kinzhal missiles and heightened air alerts nationwide.
key_points: Russia fired two Oryshik ballistic missiles on 24 May, one hitting Kyiv region and another landing in occupied Donetsk (Source: @UkraineNow/81496).
Ukrainian artillery from RSZV struck a children's playground in Kherson, killing a father and wounding a mother and two young daughters (Source: @UkraineNow/81498).

Multiple UAVs were sighted over Zaporizhzhia, Chernihiv, Chernobyl, Zhytomyr‑Rivne, and northern Kyiv regions, indicating widespread drone activity (Source: @kpszsu/62991, @kpszsu/62982, @kpszsu/62977, @kpszsu/62984, @kpszsu/62955).
A MiG‑31K took off from Savatske airfield carrying a Kh‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile, prompting a nationwide air‑raid alert (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67241, @eRadarrua/93910).
High tactical aviation activity (five units) was reported over the Baranivka area, suggesting intensified air operations (Source: @eRadarrua/93939).
Shahed drones were observed over Samara/Dnipro, near Aviatorske and Kamyanske, indicating continued low‑altitude strike threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67236, @mon1tor_ua/67237).
A drone attack on Odessa damaged residential buildings, a post office branch and a food store, injuring four people (Source: @UkraineNow/81489).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched two Oryshik ballistic missiles on 24 May, striking Kyiv region and occupied Donetsk, while Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions across the front lines. Heavy artillery hit a children's playground in Kherson, causing civilian casualties, and Shahed drones were observed over several central locations. Tactical aviation activity increased, including MiG‑31K flights carrying Kinzhal missiles and heightened air alerts nationwide.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported that Russian artillery from a multiple launch rocket system (RSZV) hit a children's playground in Kherson, killing a father and wounding a mother and two young daughters, with additional injured persons taken to hospital (Source: @UkraineNow/81498).
In Odessa, a hostile drone strike damaged residential houses, a New Post office branch and a food store, injuring four civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81489).

Ukrainian ground forces observed Shahed loitering munitions over Samara/Dnipro and near Aviatorske and Kamyanske, indicating ongoing low‑altitude threats in central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67236, @mon1tor_ua/67237).

Ukrainian air‑defence reported a nationwide alert after a MiG‑31K took off from Savatske airfield carrying a Kh‑47M2 Kinzhal missile, highlighting concerns over hypersonic strike capabilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67241, @eRadarrua/93910).
Ukrainian intelligence noted a seven‑kilometre stretch of the Odesa‑Reni highway littered with abandoned cars, suggesting civilian evacuation or desertion patterns (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30464).

On 24 May Russia launched two Oryshik ballistic missiles during a mass attack, one striking the Kyiv region and another landing on occupied Donetsk territory; a prior confirmed impact was in Bila Tserkva (Source: @UkraineNow/81496).
Russian forces continue to employ multiple launch rocket systems (RSZV) against civilian infrastructure, as evidenced by the Kherson playground attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81498).
Russia announced that the United States denied a visa to the Russian Deputy Foreign Minister, reflecting diplomatic friction (Source: @UkraineNow/81486).
Russian aviation permitted Air Tanzania to operate flights between Dar‑es‑Salaam and Moscow, indicating broader strategic air‑link developments (Source: @tass_agency/377815).
Russia and Afghanistan signed a military‑technical cooperation agreement at an international security forum in Russia (Source: @tass_agency/377801).
UAV activity was reported over Zaporizhzhia near Ternewate‑Novomykolivka, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions in the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/62991).
A UAV from the Black Sea was observed heading toward Chornomorske, indicating possible maritime‑to‑land operations in the southeast (Source: @kpszsu/62980).
High tactical aviation presence (five units) was noted over the Baranivka area, a locality within the eastern operational zone (Source: @eRadarrua/93939).
A ballistic threat from the south‑east direction was announced, highlighting potential missile trajectories toward eastern territories (Source: @eRadarrua/93913).
UAVs were sighted near Chernobyl heading west, potentially crossing the eastern front line (Source: @kpszsu/62977).
UAVs were observed over northern Kyiv region heading toward Ivankiv and Krasatychi, indicating incursions on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/62955).
A UAV from Zhytomyr moved toward Rivne in the direction of Sarney, crossing the north‑west axis (Source: @kpszsu/62984).
UAV activity was reported in Chernihiv region near Berezna, moving southward, showing north‑central pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62982).
Tactical aviation activity was reported over Baranivka, a location within the northern operational area (Source: @eRadarrua/93939).
A MiG‑31K launch from Savatske airfield, located in the north‑west, raised alerts across the country (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67241).
A UAV from the Black Sea heading to Chornomorske was noted, indicating southern maritime‑to‑land operations (Source: @kpszsu/62980).
Shahed drones were observed over Dnipro and nearby areas, suggesting southern central strike activity (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67236, @mon1tor_ua/67237).
A drone attack on Odessa caused civilian injuries and property damage, reflecting ongoing southern front threats (Source: @UkraineNow/81489).
Ballistic missile threats from the south‑east direction were highlighted, pointing to potential strikes on southern regions (Source: @eRadarrua/93913).
UAV activity near Chernobyl heading west could affect southern approaches to the front line (Source: @kpszsu/62977).
Russia launched two Oryshik ballistic missiles on 24 May, striking Kyiv region and occupied Donetsk, demonstrating continued strategic missile use (Source: @UkraineNow/81496).
A MiG‑31K carrying a Kh‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile took off from Savatske airfield, prompting a nationwide air‑raid alert (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67241, @eRadarrua/93910).
High tactical aviation activity (five units) was reported over Baranivka, indicating intensified air operations in that sector (Source: @eRadarrua/93939).

Multiple UAV sightings across Zaporizhzhia, Chernihiv, Chernobyl, Zhytomyr‑Rivne, and northern Kyiv regions show extensive drone deployment by Russian forces (Source: @kpszsu/62991, @kpszsu/62982, @kpszsu/62977, @kpszsu/62984, @kpszsu/62955).

Shahed loitering munitions were observed over central locations such as Samara/Dnipro and near Aviatorske, reflecting low‑altitude aerial threat persistence (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67236, @mon1tor_ua/67237).

sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile and drone deployments continue to target both civilian infrastructure and strategic locations across Ukraine, with ballistic strikes reaching Kyiv and occupied Donetsk and UAVs penetrating deep into eastern and northern sectors. The introduction of hypersonic-capable platforms such as the MiG‑31K with Kinzhal missiles elevates the aerial threat, prompting nationwide alerts. Shahed loitering munitions remain a persistent low‑altitude danger, especially around central urban centers, complicating air‑defence allocations.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have intensified air‑defence alerts and reported increased tactical aviation activity, while local administrations document civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Regional governors and emergency services are coordinating evacuations and medical response, particularly in Kherson and Odessa. International partners are monitoring missile use and diplomatic tensions, as evidenced by statements on visa denials and cooperation agreements.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source UAV sightings provide a granular picture of Russian reconnaissance and strike patterns, useful for predicting future target zones. The confirmed use of Oryshik missiles underscores Russia's willingness to employ strategic ballistic assets against Ukrainian territory. Monitoring of hypersonic missile deployments is critical, given their speed and limited interception windows, necessitating updated radar and early‑warning protocols.
